title: ParseCheckResult
description: Audit artifact for Phase B.6.5 of Step 8 codegen: language-native parse gate. Runs `ast.parse` for Python and shells to `tsc --noEmit --isolatedModules --skipLibCheck`, `node --check`, or `javac` for the other supported languages. Complements Phase B.6 by catching the class of failure the type-checker cannot even reach (the file's first byte isn't valid for the target language). Persisted to `artifacts/step08/parse-check-result.json` on every run, including skipped cases (opt-out flags, no in-scope files). Caller uses `in_scope_errors > 0` post-fixer to decide whether to fail Step 8; when the ONLY backend that ran was the regex smoke check (`degraded_languages` non-empty) and it fired a violation, the step fails LOUD with a `missing_tools`-based message rather than trusting the regex.

ran.description: True iff the phase actually walked the qtea file set. False only when an opt-out env var (`QTEA_SKIP_PARSE_CHECK=1` / `QTEA_NO_PARSE_CHECK=1`) was set â€” `skipped_reason` then carries the cause.

duration_s.description: Wall-clock seconds from phase entry to result construction.

files_checked.description: Number of files for which at least one backend produced a verdict. Files whose extension has no mapping (e.g. `.txt`) are excluded from this count.

in_scope_errors.description: Count of parse-error violations (equal to the length of `violations[]`). This is what the caller uses to decide whether to invoke the autofix pass / fail the step.

degraded_languages.description: Languages for which every native backend was unavailable and the check fell back to the regex smoke test. Non-empty means the gate is running in reduced-confidence mode; if `in_scope_errors > 0` for a file in one of these languages, the caller should fail loud with a `missing_tools`-based message rather than trusting the regex.

missing_tools.description: Names of executables the gate looked for but did not find (e.g. `tsc`, `node`, `javac`). Surfaced to the operator when the gate fails so they know exactly what to install.

autofix_attempted.description: True iff `codegen-violation-fixer` was invoked on the parse-error violations. Mirrors Phase B.5/B.6's single-retry policy: at most one fixer pass per step run.

post_fix_errors.description: Parse-error count after the fixer pass and a re-run of the gate. Only meaningful when `autofix_attempted=true`. Non-zero here triggers `StepResult(success=False)` and escalates to the global step retry.

file.description: SUT-relative posix path of the file that was checked.

backend_used.description: Which backend produced the verdict. `ast.parse` = Python (stdlib); `tsc` = TypeScript compiler; `node --check` = Node native syntax check; `javac` = Java compiler; `regex-smoke` = fallback regex check when no native backend was available.

ran.description: True iff at least one backend produced a verdict for this file.

ok.description: True iff the file parsed / smoke-checked without violation.

error_line.description: 1-based line number of the first error the backend reported. Null when the backend cannot localise (e.g. file-level read error).

error_message.description: Backend's error message, capped at 280 chars.

skipped_reason.description: Populated when `ran=false` at the per-file level (currently unused; reserved for future per-file skip conditions).

violations.description: Every parse-error violation the gate produced, in the same shape the `codegen-violation-fixer` agent consumes. `rule` is always `"parse-error"`.

snippet.description: The backend's error message, capped at 280 chars.
